ks1.objective: Dribble your ball through as many cone gates as you can. Use your hands or feet.
successCriteria: Keeps the ball close while moving
Goes through different gates each time
Looks up to choose a gate

lks2.objective: Develop dribbling with control — choose colour-coded gates and adjust speed for traffic and tight gaps.
successCriteria: Slows for tight gaps, speeds up in open space
Picks the next gate before arriving

uks2.objective: Scan, choose and dribble at pace — track unused gates and adapt speed and direction under blocker pressure.
successCriteria: Beats own count round on round
Uses turns to escape blocked gates
Heads up frequently while in control

space.description: Open area with many small cone gates spread out at random angles. Cross-discipline — works with foot dribble, hand dribble or hockey stick.

steps: Create lots of small cone gates within the area using different colours.
Each child has a ball (or hockey stick).
On 'GO!', children dribble through as many gates as they can in 90 seconds.
Each gate counts as one point. Same gate can be used again but try not to repeat.
Children try to beat their own score in a second round.
questions: What are you looking for whilst dribbling?

step.space: Change spacing between gates — closer for tight control, wider for speed
step.task: Children can only dribble through certain coloured gates
step.equipment: Dribble by stick, hand or feet
step.people: Add a blocker to restrict space
